scheme_name: Haryana State Meritorious Incentive Scheme for the Students Who Attain First Position in CBSE Annual Exams in the State
page_title: Haryana State Meritorious Incentive Scheme for the Students Who Attain First Position in CBSE Annual Exams in the State

details: The scheme "Haryana State Meritorious Incentive Scheme" by the Department of Higher Education, Government of Haryana, aims to encourage meritorious students who attain top positions in CBSE annual exams by providing them with financial incentives. Selected students receive an annual incentive covering their school/college fees up to the completion of their graduation. The objective of the scheme is to encourage academic excellence among students by providing financial incentives to CBSE toppers.; support students in pursuing higher education without financial constraints; and reward merit and performance in CBSE Class 10th and 12th board examinations.
benefits: The entire tuition fee is covered, up to a maximum limit.
Up to ₹25,000/- per year till 10+2 level and up to ₹1,00,000/- per year till graduation.
Up to ₹1,00,000/- per year till graduation.
The incentive continues until the completion of graduation.
eligibility: The applicant should be a domicile of Haryana.
The applicant should be the top-ranked boy or girl in the CBSE 10th or 12th annual exams at the state level.
The applicant should be continuing his/her education as a regular student.
The applicant should have passed from a school in Haryana.
For 10th Class, the applicant should have secured 92% or above in English, Science & Technology, Mathematics, Social Science, and the best one of the remaining subjects.
For the 12th Class, the applicant should have secured 90% or above in English and the four best subjects in Science (Medical & Non-Medical), Commerce, or Humanities.

online: of the "Centralized Scholarship Portal" of the Department of Higher Education, Haryana.
On the next page, provide your Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID) and click "Search".

*Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) is mandatory for availing of any Scholarship benefit. Students are advised to ensure their all particulars such as Name, DOB, Aadhar No. etc are fully updated in PPP before applying for any scholarship scheme.

A List of the members of your family will be displayed. Select the checkbox against your name and click "Generate OTP". Enter the OTP received on your registered Mobile Number and click on "Verify OTP".
After OTP verification, you will be taken to the registration page. In the registration form, all the mandatory fields get auto-filled. You only need to cross-check the information.
Select your Department and College. Create a Password and click "Register".
In the right corner of the menu ribbon, click "
". Use the username and password created during your registration to log in.

Navigate to the online form of the scheme you intend to apply for. In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections.
Click "Apply" to submit your application. You'll receive a confirmation message.

documents_required: Admission proof for higher education (10+2 or graduation level).
Fee receipt from the educational institution.
An affidavit stating non-receipt of any other scholarship/stipend.
Recommendation letter from the Head of Institution.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Haryana State Meritorious Incentive Scheme?
faqs.answer: This scheme is for Haryana domicile students who secure the top position in CBSE Class 10th or 12th annual exams at the state level.

faqs.question: What benefits does the scheme provide to CBSE toppers?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers the entire tuition fees up to graduation, with a limit of ₹25,000 per year till 10+2 and ₹1,00,000 per year till graduation.

faqs.question: How can students apply for this incentive?
faqs.answer: Students must submit their applications through the Head of their Institution after securing the required percentage in CBSE exams.

faqs.question: Can a student receive this incentive along with another scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, students availing this scheme are not eligible for any other scholarship or stipend from the state government or any other source.

faqs.question: What documents are required for application?
faqs.answer: Students must submit their domicile certificate, CBSE marksheet, admission proof, fee receipt, bank details, affidavit of non-receipt of other scholarships, and a recommendation letter.

faqs.question: What happens if a student fails a class during the incentive period?
faqs.answer: Students who fail or have a compartment in any subject are disqualified from receiving further incentives under this scheme.

faqs.question: How are the top position holders determined?
faqs.answer: For Class 10th, students securing 92% or above in specified subjects are considered. For Class 12th, students securing 90% or above in English and the best four subjects are considered.

faqs.question: When is the last date to apply for the incentive?
faqs.answer: Students should check with their respective institutions for the application deadline, which follows CBSE result announcements.

faqs.question: Can students pursuing diploma courses avail this incentive?
faqs.answer: No, only students pursuing regular higher secondary and degree-level courses are eligible.

faqs.question: Is the incentive available for postgraduate studies?
faqs.answer: No, the incentive is only available up to graduation level.

faqs.question: Where can students check their application status?
faqs.answer: Students can check their application status through their institution or the Higher Education Department’s official portal.
